verses.text: Howl, for 'the day of the LORD is near,' a cloudy day, the time of the heathen's judgment. The day dawns. God has an hour appointed for every proud nation.
Howl, for 'the day of the LORD is near,' a cloudy day, the time of the heathen's judgment. The day dawns. God has an hour appointed for every proud nation.
Howl, for 'the day of the LORD is near,' a cloudy day, the time of the heathen's judgment. The day dawns. God has an hour appointed for every proud nation.
The sword shall fall on Egypt, and her allies — Ethiopia, Libya, and others — shall fall with her. Allies share her doom. Those who stand with the doomed share their fall.
The sword shall fall on Egypt, and her allies — Ethiopia, Libya, and others — shall fall with her. Allies share her doom. Those who stand with the doomed share their fall.
All who uphold Egypt shall fall; the land shall be desolate, and they shall know the LORD. Props removed. Every support of the proud collapses together.
All who uphold Egypt shall fall; the land shall be desolate, and they shall know the LORD. Props removed. Every support of the proud collapses together.
All who uphold Egypt shall fall; the land shall be desolate, and they shall know the LORD. Props removed. Every support of the proud collapses together.
Messengers shall terrify Ethiopia, and Nebuchadnezzar shall end the multitude of Egypt. Fear spreads. The news of judgment shakes the surrounding nations.
Messengers shall terrify Ethiopia, and Nebuchadnezzar shall end the multitude of Egypt. Fear spreads. The news of judgment shakes the surrounding nations.
He and his people, 'the terrible of the nations,' shall waste the land; God will dry the rivers and sell it to the wicked. Strength withdrawn. The very Nile they trusted is dried at God's word.
He and his people, 'the terrible of the nations,' shall waste the land; God will dry the rivers and sell it to the wicked. Strength withdrawn. The very Nile they trusted is dried at God's word.
He will destroy the idols of Noph and pour His fury on Sin and No. Idols toppled. The gods of Egypt cannot save her cities from the LORD.
He will destroy the idols of Noph and pour His fury on Sin and No. Idols toppled. The gods of Egypt cannot save her cities from the LORD.
He will destroy the idols of Noph and pour His fury on Sin and No. Idols toppled. The gods of Egypt cannot save her cities from the LORD.
He will set fire in Egypt; Sin shall writhe, No be torn open, and her young men fall by the sword. Cities in anguish. The land's pride and youth perish together.
He will set fire in Egypt; Sin shall writhe, No be torn open, and her young men fall by the sword. Cities in anguish. The land's pride and youth perish together.
At Tehaphnehes the day shall darken when He breaks Egypt's yoke; thus He executes judgments. Power broken. God dims the glory of the oppressor.
At Tehaphnehes the day shall darken when He breaks Egypt's yoke; thus He executes judgments. Power broken. God dims the glory of the oppressor.
God says, 'I have broken the arm of Pharaoh,' and it shall not be bound up to heal. Strength shattered. The arm that smote others is itself disabled.
God says, 'I have broken the arm of Pharaoh,' and it shall not be bound up to heal. Strength shattered. The arm that smote others is itself disabled.
He will break both Pharaoh's arms and 'strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon,' putting His sword in his hand. Power transferred. God arms whom He wills and disarms whom He wills.
He will break both Pharaoh's arms and 'strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon,' putting His sword in his hand. Power transferred. God arms whom He wills and disarms whom He wills.
He will break both Pharaoh's arms and 'strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon,' putting His sword in his hand. Power transferred. God arms whom He wills and disarms whom He wills.
He will hold up Babylon's arms while Pharaoh's fall, and scatter the Egyptians among the nations. The verdict stands. God alone lifts up one and brings down another.
He will hold up Babylon's arms while Pharaoh's fall, and scatter the Egyptians among the nations. The verdict stands. God alone lifts up one and brings down another.
